вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡івЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡№вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡·вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡өвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹ рџ‡ІвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡ҒвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡ёвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡ёвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡¦вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡¬вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹рџ‡ҒвЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹вЂ‹

Leyndell, the Royal Capital of the Lands Between.

Left forgotten and abandoned, desolate as the wars had ravaged the once glorious crumbling buildings. Melina's footsteps echoed in the empty area, her gaze wandering around the ruined market stalls. The cloth fabric intended to keep the rain off, now tattered and worn with holes and faulty supports. Her dark colored cloak swished around at the bottom with every step as she walked with the Tarnished. "Are you prepared to commit a cardinal sin, dear Tarnished?" Her soft tone underplayed the gravity of the situation, the unavoidable fact that the Erdtree was demanded to burn.

"YouвЂ™ve grown much stronger since when we first met. I am certain that you will be able to mend the remains of the Elden Ring." Melina smiled, doing anything to really lessen the atmosphere of the situation. Pebbles kicked occasionally under feet, skidding across the ground and bouncing to a stop. There was hardly anything interesting to look at anymore, the lifts and doors of Leyndell hardly even working. It was a wonder that they had managed to get this far without any major roadblocks or obstacles in the terrain.
